qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CMS media gallery thumbnail path traversal discloses images outside media/wysiwyg
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns admin CMS thumbnail path traversal in `Cms/Wysiwyg/ImagesController::thumbnailAction()` and `Mage_Cms_Model_Wysiwyg_Images_Storage::resizeOnTheFly()`, not the advisory's customer file-upload blocklist bypass in `Mage_Catalog_Model_Product_Option_Type_File::_validateUploadedFile()`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding targets a different OpenMage code path and root cause than the advisory. It demonstrates admin-only thumbnail traversal and image disclosure, not the advisory's incomplete extension blocklist in customer option file upload handling.
evidence: The advisory identifies `app/code/core/Mage/Catalog/Model/Product/Option/Type/File.php:230-237` and `app/code/core/Mage/Catalog/etc/config.xml:824`, where `_validateUploadedFile()` applies `ExcludeExtension` to only `php,exe` instead of the broader protected extension set.

The qualifying finding identifies `app/code/core/Mage/Adminhtml/controllers/Cms/Wysiwyg/ImagesController.php:173-179` and `app/code/core/Mage/Cms/Model/Wysiwyg/Images/Storage.php:376-429`, where `thumbnailAction()` accepts a user-controlled encoded path and `resizeOnTheFly()` opens a traversed image path outside `media/wysiwyg`.

The finding requires a low-privilege admin account with `cms/media_gallery` access and yields image disclosure, while the advisory describes customer-controlled upload of alternative PHP-executable extensions into `media/custom_options/quote/` with possible RCE on permissive web-server configurations.
